title: pi-workflows parallel step creates all sub-steps eagerly at parse; Claude Code parallel(thunks) defers execution until each thunk is invoked allowing conditional branching where some thunks never run; pi cannot express conditional-fan-out without per-sub-step 'when' fields
description: Per R-0014 recon axis 2: Claude Code workflows' parallel(thunks: Array<() => Promise<T>>) takes lazy thunks; some thunks may never execute based on prior-stage results. pi-workflows parallel step declares all sub-steps upfront in the YAML spec; all are created eagerly (even unreachable ones via gate breaks). Operators wanting conditional fan-out (e.g., 'run fast-path or thorough-path based on input.mode') must hand-build with gate routing rather than declaring naturally.

evidence.reference: Part 3 axis 2 — parallel() with Thunks vs parallel Step Type — INSPIRE verdict
parallel sub-step eager declaration; no per-sub-step conditional

impact: Operators expressing conditional fan-out patterns add complexity via gate steps + multiple parallel blocks. Simple lazy-thunk semantics from Claude Code workflows are inexpressible at the spec level. Spec becomes verbose for what should be a single 'parallel + when' construct.
canonical_vocabulary: thunk-parallel; lazy sub-step evaluation; conditional fan-out; per-sub-step 'when' field
proposed_resolution: Add 'when: <expression>' field to parallel sub-step definitions. Executor evaluates each sub-step's 'when' before dispatch; skips false-when sub-steps without creating their subprocess. Alternative: new 'thunk-parallel' step type that takes an array of step definitions each with optional 'when'. Simpler addition: extend existing parallel sub-step grammar with optional 'when' — matches the 'when' surface already used at top-level step + on transform/gate/loop steps.
